what_it_does: Defines a standalone HTML render proof that loads shared styles and runtime scripts, fetches a session-card archetype from /dna/layouts.json, adapts two raw session-shaped records, and mounts two rendered portrait session cards into separate containers.
observations: The page title names the proof as FACE-1 session-card render proof.
Two mount containers are sized to 380px and displayed in a wrapped flex body.
The script fetches /dna/layouts.json and selects layouts.archetypes["session-card"].
Two raw session records are declared with id, name, title, cwd, state, and last_activity fields; one also includes model.
Both records are passed through DNA.faceRecord.sessionRecord before rendering.
Each card is rendered through DNA.renderArchetype with portrait orientation and a different visualDevice organism.
standouts: The file is structured as a live render proof rather than a static markup mock.
Comments explicitly frame session-card as a decision-card sibling bound through slot_map to session fields.
The proof checks two session states and two organism visuals in one page.
conventions: Uses external shared CSS and runtime scripts instead of inline component implementation.
Uses an async IIFE for setup and mounting.
Keeps the archetype external by fetching the layout registry rather than defining the layout inline.
Uses host-shaped raw data followed by a DNA adapter step before rendering.
concerns: The script assumes fetch, the DNA global, the layouts response shape, and required archetype key are all present without error handling.
The Google Fonts dependency and absolute /surface and /dna paths make the proof dependent on a specific served environment.
The inline comments are dense and carry architectural claims that the page itself only partially demonstrates.
notes: A small browser proof page for rendering two adapted session records through the session-card archetype.
questions: What exact fields does the session-card slot_map require beyond the raw session fields shown here?
Is this proof intended to run only from a particular server root that provides /surface and /dna paths?
What failure state should be shown if the archetype or DNA runtime is unavailable?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated render-proof purpose matches the scripted behavior: it adapts real-shaped session records and renders them through the fetched session-card archetype.
intention_signals: The file seems intended to demonstrate that live-shaped session data can be adapted and rendered through the same archetype mechanism used by the surrounding FACE-1 surface.
novelty: It treats an HTML proof page as a direct exercise of the DNA archetype pipeline, including adapter shaping and organism slot injection.
contribution: Provides a concrete session-card render fixture with two sample records, adapter usage, fetched archetype wiring, and mounted visual-device variants that a fused system would keep as a compact proof of session-record rendering behavior.
summary_for_embedding: Standalone HTML proof that fetches the session-card archetype, adapts two raw session records, and renders two portrait cards with different DNA organism visuals.
intention_for_embedding: Created to verify that session-shaped host data binds cleanly into the session-card archetype through the DNA face-record adapter and render pipeline.
